description: basic dual-return from user functions (safe_div pattern)
steps: safe_div success: result=[5], err=[0]
safe_div division-by-zero guarded: result=[], err=[1]
Full roadmap errors.slop example end-to-end
Function wrapping <. (file read) re-returns error on missing file
Function wrapping <. re-returns success on existing file
Function wrapping to_num re-returns error on invalid string
Function wrapping to_num re-returns success on valid string
Function calling another user function and propagating its error

description: chained error propagation through multiple function levels
steps: Three-level chain: step3 calls step2 calls step1, error propagates from step1
Three-level chain: success path flows through all levels
Error code value preserved through chain (not just [1])

description: mixed error and success paths in sequence
steps: Same function called with valid then invalid input
Same function called with invalid then valid input
Multiple calls in sequence with alternating success/failure
Read nonexistent file returns error code [1]
Write then read round-trip returns success with correct data
File read error data is empty string
Valid integer string returns (number, [0])
Valid float string returns (number, [0])
Invalid string returns ([], [1])
Empty string returns ([], [1])

description: division by zero guarded vs unguarded
steps: Guarded: user checks b==[0] before dividing, returns error code
Unguarded: direct a/[0] causes runtime panic (exit code != 0)

description: empty array and edge cases in error patterns
steps: Return [] as error result, check it is falsy
Return [0] as error code, verify == [0] is truthy
Return [1] as error code, verify != [0] is truthy
Error code in if/else branches to different output
Loop processing items, count failures via error codes
Early return on first error in a processing pipeline

description: runtime panics for unguarded errors remain intact
steps: Unguarded division by zero panics
Type mismatch in arithmetic panics
Array length mismatch in arithmetic panics
Multi-element comparison panics
